compatibility_report:
Elden Ring:  EAC fails to load, gives Launch Error:  "Unexpected Error:  #1"

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/7452.
@ConHuevosGuey posted on 2024-01-28T15:30:24:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:  Elden Ring
- Steam AppID of the game:

## System Information
- Distrobution:  Slackware 15.0
- GPU:  Nvidia RTX 3090ti
- Video driver version: Nvidia 545.29.06
- Kernel version: 6.1.66
- Proton version:  Proton Experimental (Tried all available versions as well)

## I confirm:
- [X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14076553/steam-1245620.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When launching the game the EAC popup occurs, attempts to load then has the title "Launch Error"  with "Unexpected Error: #1" and the game must exit.

## Reproduction
Launch the game.

##  Comments

I can find no other error logs as to why EAC keeps faiiling when loading.  If I use the flatpak version of steam, the game loads fine and EAC loads correctly and I can play online using Steam's flatpak version.  The game ran perfectly fine, including loading EAC when glibc 2.33 was installed.  However after updating glibc I continued to keep getting error #1 on the EAC loading banner.  I've confirmed with others that the game does indeed work with my current version of glibc (2.38), however they are Arch and Nobara distrobutions.

I'm unsure what the cause of this issue as I cannot find any information on error #1 or how to further troubleshoot it.  Nothing appears to fail in the logs and it simply seems to be the EAC module failing to load correctly for some reason. posted at

issue_title: Power Rangers: Battle for the Grid (1110100)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Power Rangers: Battle for the Grid 
- Steam AppID of the game: 1110100

## System Information
Steam Deck

- Proton version: Proton 7.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms

Story mode cinematics replaced with SMPTE color bars. 

## Reproduction

Run the game, enter and play through story mode.

Despite game being marked Steam Deck verified, story mode cinematics represented as color bars. **Initial intro cinematic might be displayed correctly, but those which come further down the road aren't.**

I see the specific issue was addressed here #5881 and cinematics indeed are working on my Linux Desktop, but not on Deck.

MTG Arena now crashes, used to run fine.

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/7212.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Total War: Pharoah Dynasties
- Steam AppID of the game: 2951630

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU/PCIe/SSE2
- Video driver version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 580.82.09
- Kernel version: 6.16.7-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [link](https://gist.github.com/DanZanzibar/4f0a9a681be06e73bb5becfbc7ad3ad2)
- Proton version: 1749140930 proton-9.0-4f

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2951630.log.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22410237/steam-2951630.log.zip)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When in battles, frame rates drop to <5, presumably due to this shader compilation error that keeps showing up in the proton log:

```
88933.747:02fc:0300:warn:seh:OutputDebugStringW L"Direct3D failed to compile VS 'vs40_shadow_4_bones_dismember' in file 'FX/WeightedWithBlood.hlsl'\n\tfull path: 'FX/WeightedWithBlood.hlsl'\n\tError: error X3501: 'vs40_shadow_4_bones_dismember': entrypoint not found\n\n"
```

The menus and campaign map do not suffer from this issue at all.

Many proton versions have been tried, including GE-proton. Always the same issue.


## Reproduction
Start game, go into "Options", then "benchmark", then "battle benchmark".

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Pickup Basketball VR
- Steam AppID of the game: 1475200

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.4
- Kernel version: 5.13.1-178-tkg-bmq
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/a69ef95b1b481d585b42f7796cfb0204
- Proton version: 6.3-5, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

This game suffered a regression. Previously, it would work out of the box with Proton. By running it and reading steam-1475200.log, we saw that it needed api-ms-win-core-console-l1-2-0.dll.
@EsdrasTarsis obtained the stubfile offering the functions for this DLL from a wine [commit](https://source.winehq.org/git/wine.git/commit/24aba09015eb3ecbce99d148ac4b48cb173aba80) from Piotr Caban and recompiled in a build that I tested, but then another error appeared for library api-ms-win-core-kernel32-legacy-l1-1-5.dll. He also made a second build and the missing library this time would be api-ms-win-core-console-l3-2-0.dll. This one, however, is undocumented, so he had to go through some hoops and jumps to get the functions it provided, but he also made a third stub and a third build, which then worked.

This bug report thus will provide the solution to this problem. @EsdrasTarsis will upload his patch.

## Reproduction
Try and start the game with regular Proton 6.3-5 or Proton Experimental. It won't start.

No steam log on this case because the issue already has a solution.

issue_title: VRR locked at 60hz on Source 2013 games on Proton 9 and Experimental
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Source SDK 2013 Games (Tested: Team Fortress 2 Classic, Half-Life 2, Fortress Forever 2013 Branch)
- Steam AppID of the game: 220, 253530, 243750

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7600
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.1.0-devel (git-79c84e4b9c) [Also observed on other Mesa versions]
- Kernel version: 6.13.7 (also observed on earlier kernel versions)
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/webbongithub/1f6c957b248f919a2a9152ee41e0e9ad
- Proton version: 9.0-4, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. [This affects many games and I haven't found this specific issue]
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When Vsync is enabled on a VRR monitor, the FPS will never go above sixty. VRR /seems to work/ given it goes below 60 at times. This issue was observed on GNOME and KDE. Using Proton 8 or below resolves the issue.

<details>
<summary>Screenshots of the issue</summary>

## Proton 9
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4880b00-a042-4b25-9223-480e8f9ef006)

## Proton 8
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b49d0888-6d57-4b30-abbe-2224618641b9)
</details>

## Reproduction

1. Launch a Source 2013 game with a VRR monitor on Proton 9 or Experimental
2. Enable Vsync
3. Profit

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Sword Art Online: Integral Factor
- Steam AppID of the game: 2371630

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Vega 64
- Video driver version: 4.6 Mesa 23.1.6
- Kernel version: `6.4.12-1-MANJARO`
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [gist](https://gist.github.com/hasezoey/5d0240106768eafd05571b3f32e613a3)
- Proton version: 8.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2371630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12466431/steam-2371630.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Unable to get into the game again, because of infinite loading of what (probably) should be a ingame browser window (according to some discussions, i dont know if that is correct)

## Reproduction

- start the game
- click to start the actual game ("Link Start")
- wait for the loading bar to finish
- observe "loading circle" still being there and text under the progress bar to read `Ready. Close the notice to start`
- wait some time
- observe nothing changing

Additional Context:
this issue seems to also happen to steam-deck users, but they seemingly can if it by moving the game from the sd-card to the internal storage

for me the game lives on a btrfs disk (including the compatdata)

also likely related to the "Ready. Close the notice to start" issue seems to be that the ingame shop seemingly never loads any data (which is the original reason for me to have restarted the game)

Note: i also tried not having a controller connected, in case this should change, but nothing changed

screenshot:
![Screenshot_20230829_174425](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/10911626/b4d3dccf-459e-47c9-b08e-c3f2a8c61565)
(Note the "X" in the top right does nothing when clicked-on)

issue_title: All Games Fail to Launch Through Protons Version >7
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: All Games, a log of Super Monkey Ball Banana Mania is presented as an example
- Steam AppID of the game: All, 1316910 is presented as an example

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1080 Mobile
- Video driver version: 560.28.03
- Native Vulkan Version: 1.3.283
- Mesa Version: 21.1.4
- Kernel version: 6.8.12
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [System Info](https://gist.github.com/E-Allie/eda219e1ec04c885b5285cc33c716df4)
- Proton version: >7

## I confirm:
- [✓] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [✓] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

[steam-1316910-NOTWORKING9.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16503793/steam-1316910-NOTWORKING9.log)
[steam-1316910-WORKING7.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16503794/steam-1316910-WORKING7.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
On all proton versions greater than 7, all Windows games fail to launch. A black window opens up for a brief moment, and then closes

When going through the log, I found that this could be the primary error/point of divergence between Proton 7 [working], and those greater than that, found on line 5566:

`83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault code=c0000005 flags=0 addr=(nil) ip=0 tid=01c0
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  info[0]=0000000000000008
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  info[1]=0000000000000000
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  rax=000000000000008f rbx=00000001222fe1a0 rcx=0000000000000000 rdx=0000000000000002
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  rsi=00000001222fe1b0 rdi=00005555931ceda0 rbp=00000001222fa130 rsp=00000001222fa118
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault   r8=0000000000000002  r9=0000000000000092 r10=0000000000000000 r11=000000000000007f
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  r12=00000001222fe1b0 r13=00005555931ceda0 r14=0000000000000223 r15=00000001222fa140
83586.114:0124:01c0:warn:seh:handle_syscall_fault backtrace: --- Exception 0xc0000005 at (nil):.
83586.114:0124:01c0:warn:seh:dump_syscall_fault backtrace: returning to user mode ip=00006ffff950e60a ret=c0000005
83586.114:0124:01c0:err:msvcrt:_wassert (L"!status && \"vkGetPhysicalDeviceSurfaceCapabilitiesKHR\"",L"../src-wine/dlls/winevulkan/loader_thunks.c",5115)`

The comparable line in the working log is 2779


## Reproduction

Freshly install Nvidia drivers, steam and proton.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Throne and Liberty
- Steam AppID of the game: 2429640

ID: com.valvesoftware.Steam

Arch: x86_64
Branch: stable
Version: 1.0.0.79
License: LicenseRef-proprietary
Origin: flathub
Collection: org.flathub.Stable
Installation: system
Installed: 45,5 MB
Runtime: org.freedesktop.Platform/x86_64/23.08
Sdk: org.freedesktop.Sdk/x86_64/23.08


## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

With Proton Experimental, the game starts and runs good so far (have completed the Tutorial and got to the first city port).

One issue I have is that the game says Dualsense controller supported, however when the controller is connected wireless via Bluetooth, it is not recognized / not working in-game. This is both with Steam Input enabled or disabled.

But when connected wired via USB, then it works (button icons are OK).

EDIT1:
Using the Dualsense controller via Bluetooth, with Steam Input disabled and changing the registry:
DisableHidraw=1
Enable SDL=0

Results in Wine using the controller as Xinput in Wine / Control Panel / Game Controllers and the game then detects the controller as Xbox and works, the button icons are Xbox styled.
